questions.subject: Physics

questions.q: Which of the following velocity-time graphs shows a realistic situation for a body in motion?
questions.solution: In the other graphs, at a particular time more than one velocity is shown which is not possible in realistic situation.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A body starting from rest moves along a straight line with a constant acceleration. The variation of speed (v) with distance $(s)$ is represented by the graph
questions.solution:
For a body moving with constant acceleration $a, v=u+a t$ Since the body starts from rest, $u=0$
$$
\therefore \quad v=a t
$$
which is a straight line passing through the origin. Hence the correct graph is (2).
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A body is projected horizontally with a velocity of $4 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{sec}$. The velocity of the body after 0.7 seconds will be nearly (Take $g=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{sec}^2$ )
questions.solution:
After $0.7 \mathrm{sec}$ the horizontal velocity component of the body $=4 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{sec}$ since there is no change in velocity in horizontal direction.
The vertical velocity component after 0.7 seconds $=0+g t=0+0.7 \times 10$
$=7 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{sec}$
$\therefore$ Resultant velocity of the body $=\sqrt{(4 \sqrt{2})^2+7^2}$ $=\sqrt{32+49}=9 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{sec}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The displacement $x$ of a particle varies with time $t$ as $x=a e^{-a t}+B^{\beta x}$, where $a, b, \alpha$ and $\beta$ are positive constants. The velocity of the particle will:
questions.solution:
From question
$\begin{aligned}
& x=a e^{-\alpha \mathrm{t}}+b e^{\beta t} \\
& V=\frac{d x}{d t}=-a \alpha e^{-x t}+b \beta_e^{\mathrm{B}+} \\
& \Rightarrow \mathrm{V}=-a \alpha e^{-t}+b \beta \varepsilon^{\beta t}
\end{aligned}$
$\therefore$ We can tell that velocity will increase with time since the negative component will decrease with an increase in time and the positive component will increase in the equation given equation.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Two bodies $\mathrm{A}$ (of mass $1 \mathrm{~kg}$ ) and $\mathrm{B}$ (of mass $3 \mathrm{~kg}$ ) are dropped from heights of $16 \mathrm{~m}$ and $25 \mathrm{~m}$, respectively. The ratio of the time taken by them to reach the ground is:
questions.solution:
We know that time taken by a body from height $h$ to reach the ground is.
$\begin{aligned}
t & =\sqrt{\frac{2 h}{g}} \\
\therefore \quad \frac{t_{\mathrm{A}}}{t_{\mathrm{B}}} & =\frac{\frac{2 h_{\mathrm{A}}}{g}}{\frac{2 h_{\mathrm{B}}}{g}}=\sqrt{\frac{h_{\mathrm{A}}}{h_{\mathrm{B}}}} \\
& =\sqrt{\frac{16}{25}}=\frac{4}{5} .
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A particle moves in a straight line with a constant acceleration. It changes its velocity from $10 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$ to $20 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$ while passing through a distance $135 \mathrm{~m}$ in t second. The value of $t$ is
questions.solution:
$a=\frac{V_f^2-V_i^2}{2 S}, t=\frac{V_f-V_i}{a}$
or $S=\frac{1}{2}(u+v) t$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A bullet emerges from a barrel of length <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> with a speed of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>640</mn><mi> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. Assuming constant acceleration, the approximate time that is spent in the barrel after the gun is fired is
questions.solution:
Given, s=1.2 m, v=640 ms-1,  a=?,  u=0;t=?

We have the third equation of motion

                      2as=v2-u2

             2a×1.2=640×640

Or                      a=8 × 64 × 1033

And by first equation of motion

                          v=u+at

Or                      t=va=154×10-3

                              =3.75×10-3s ≈4 ms
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A particle moves with constant acceleration along a straight line. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>v</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>v</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>v</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> are the average velocities in the three successive intervals <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>t</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>t</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>t</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> of time, then the correct relation is
questions.solution:
Let \(u\) be the initial velocity.
\(\therefore v_{1}=u+a t_{1}, v_{2}=u+d\left(t_{3}+t_{2}\right) \mathrm{and}\)
\(v_{3}=u+a\left(t_{1}+t_{3}+t_{3}\right)\)
Now, \(v_{1}=\frac{u w_{1}}{2}, \frac{u\left(u+a t_{1}\right)}{2}=u+\frac{1}{2}\) at,
\(v_{1}=\frac{v_{1}+c_{2}}{2}+u+a t_{1}+\frac{1}{2} a t_{2}\)
\(v_{3}=\frac{v_{2}+v_{2}}{2}=u+a t_{1}+a t_{2}+\frac{1}{2} a t_{3}\)
So, \(\left(y_{1}-v_{2}\right)=-\frac{1}{2} a\left(t_{3}+t_{2}\right)\)
and \(\left(v_{2}-v_{3}\right)=-\frac{1}{2}\) a \(\left(t_{2}+t_{3}\right)\)
\(\therefore \frac{\left(v_{1}-v_{2}\right)}{\left(v_{2}-v_{3}\right)}=\frac{\left(t_{1}+t_{2}\right)}{\left(t_{2}+t_{3}\right)}\)
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A 2 m wide truck is moving with a uniform speed v0 = 8 m s-1 along a straight horizontal road. A pedestrian starts to cross the road with a uniform speed v moving in a straight line, when the truck is 4 m away from him. The minimum value of v  so that he can cross the road safely is
questions.solution:
Let the man starts crossing the road at an angle θ shown in figure. For safe crossing the condition is that the man must cross the road by the time the truck describes the distance 4 + AC or 4 + 2 cot θ.


​ ∴ 4 + 2 cot θ 8 = 2 / sin θ v

or v = 8 2 sin θ + cos θ ...(1)

For minimum v, dv d θ = 0

or - 8 2 cos θ - sin θ ( 2 sin θ + cos θ ) 2 = 0

or                     2 cos θ - sin θ = 0

or                                   tan θ = 2



from Equation (1)

vmin=82(25)+15=85 m s-1
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The motion of a body falling from rest in a resistive medium is described by the equation <math><mfrac><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>v</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>t</mi></mrow></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mi>a</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>b</mi><mi>v</mi><mo>,</mo></math> where <math><mi>a</mi></math> and <math><mi>b</mi></math> are constants. The velocity at any time <math><mi>t</mi></math> is
questions.solution:
dvdt=a-bv

or ∫0vdva-bv=∫0tdt

or -1bloga-bv0v=t

or a-bva=e-bt

or a-bav=e-bt

v=ab(1-e-bt)
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
Given below are the equations of motion of four particles A, B, C and D
xA=6t-3;  xB=4t2-2t+3;   xC=3t3-2t2+t-7; xD=7 cos⁡60o-3sin⁡30o  

The particle moving with constant acceleration is
questions.solution:
xA=6t-3

vA=ddt6t-3=6

Particle A moves with constant velocity.

vB=ddt4t2-2t+3=8t-2,  aB=ddt8t-2=8

Particle B moves with constant acceleration

vC=ddt3t3-2t2+t-7=9t2-4t+1

aC=18t-4

So, the particle moves with variable acceleration.

xD=7cos⁡60o-3sin⁡30o 

Since xD does not depend upon time therefore particle is at rest.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q: A rocket is fired upwards. Its engine explodes fully in 12 s. The height reached by the rocket as calculated from its velocity-time graph is
questions.solution:
The height reached by rocket is equal to the area under the velocity-time graph

 h=12×132×1200

h= 79200 m

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A body starts from rest and is uniformly accelerated for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>30</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math>. The distance travelled in the first <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math> is <math><msub><mrow><mi>S</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></math>, next <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math> is <math><msub><mrow><mi>S</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> and the last 10 s is <math><msub><mrow><mi>S</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></math>. Then <math><msub><mrow><mi>S</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub><mi> </mi><mo>:</mo><mi> </mi><msub><mrow><mi>S</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi> </mi></mrow></msub><mi> </mi><mo>:</mo><mi> </mi><msub><mrow><mi>S</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></math> is the same as
options: 1 : 2 : 4
1 : 2 : 5
1 : 3 : 5
1 : 3 : 9
questions.solution:
a = constant

S1=v0t+12at2

v1=v0+at

S2=v1t+12at2 ⇒v0t+32at2

v2=v1+at

=v0+2at

S3=v2t+12at2

=v0t+52at2

v0=0

S1=12at2;       S2=32at2;      S3=52at2

S1 : S2 : S3=1 : 3 : 5
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A particle moves in a plane along an elliptic path given by $\frac{x^{2}}{a^{2}}+\frac{y^{2}}{b^{2}}=1 .$ At point $(0, b)$, the $x$-component of velocity is $\mathrm{u}$. The y-component of acceleration at this point is-
questions.solution:
$\begin{array}{l}
\frac{x^{2}}{a^{2}}+\frac{y^{2}}{b^{2}}=1 \\
u_{x}=u \text { at }(0, b) \\
u_{y}=0 \\
\frac{2 x}{a^{2}} \frac{d x}{d t}+\frac{2 y}{b^{2}} \frac{d y}{d t}=0
\end{array}$
Again diff. w.r.t. to time
$\frac{2 x}{a^{2}} \frac{d^{2} x}{d t^{2}}+\frac{2}{a^{2}}\left(\frac{d x}{d t}\right)^{2}+\begin{array}{ll}
2 y & d^{2} y \\
b^{2} & {d t^{2}}+\frac{2}{b^{2}}\left(\begin{array}{l}
d y \\
d t
\end{array}\right)=0
\end{array}$
acceleration at $(0, b)$ is
$a_{y}=\frac{-b}{a^{2}} u^{2}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
Two skaters $\mathrm{P}$ and $\mathrm{Q}$ are skating towards each other. Skater $\mathrm{P}$ throws a ball towards $\mathrm{W}$ every $5 \mathrm{~s}$ such that it always leaves her hand with speed $2 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$ with respect to the ground. Consider two cases:
$\mathrm{(I)}$ $\mathrm{P}$ runs with speed $1 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$ towards $\mathrm{Q}$ while $\mathrm{Q}$ remains stationary
$\mathrm{(II)}$ $\mathrm{Q}$ runs with speed $1 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$ towards $\mathrm{P}$ while $\mathrm{P}$ remains stationary.
Note that irrespective of speed of $\mathrm{P}$, ball always leaves $\mathrm{P's}$ hand with speed $2 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$ with respect to the ground. Ignore gravity. Balls will be received by $\mathrm{Q}$
options: one every $2.5 \mathrm{~s}$ in case $\mathrm{(I)}$ and one every $3.3 \mathrm{~s}$ in case $\mathrm{(II)}$
one every $2 \mathrm{~s}$ in case $\mathrm{(I)}$ and one every $4 \mathrm{~s}$ in case $\mathrm{(II)}$
one every $3.3 \mathrm{~s}$ in case $\mathrm{(I)}$ and one every $2.5 \mathrm{~s}$ in case $\mathrm{(II)}$
one every $2.5 \mathrm{~s}$ in case $\mathrm{(I)}$ and one every $2.5 \mathrm{~s}$ in case $\mathrm{(II)}$
questions.solution:
(I) Let initial distance between $\mathrm{P} \& \mathrm{Q}$ is $\mathrm{x}$
at $\mathrm{t}_{1}=\frac{\mathrm{x}}{2}$ a receive the ball. Next ball. $\begin{array}{l} t_{2}=\frac{x-5}{2}+5 \\ \Delta t=\frac{5}{2} \end{array}$ (II) in second case at $t=0$ $\mathrm{P}$ throws the ball.
$t_{1}=\frac{x}{3}$. Next ball $\begin{array}{l} t_{2}=\frac{x-5}{3}+5 \\ \Delta t=\frac{10}{3} \end{array}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A balloon is moving up in air vertically above a point A on the ground. When it is a height h1, a girl standing at a distance d (point B) from A (see figure) sees it at an angle 45° with respect to the vertical. When the balloon climbs up a further height h2, it is seen at an angle 60° with respect to the vertical if the girl moves further by a distance 2.464 d (point C). Then the height h2 is (given tan30°=0.5774):
questions.solution:
t45=h1d

h1=d   .......(i)

h1+h2d+2.464d=tan30°=13=0.5774

h1+h2=(3.464)d×0.5774

h1+h2=2d   .......(ii)

∴  h2=d
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A particle starts from rest and traverses a distance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></math> with uniform acceleration, then moves uniformly over a further distance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></math> and finally comes to rest after moving a further distance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></math> under uniform retardation. Assuming entire motion to be rectilinear motion, the ratio of average speed over the journey to the maximum speed on its way is
questions.solution:
Let t1,t2 and t3 be the time taken by the particles to cover the distance 2x, 4x and 6x respectively. Let v be the velocity of the particle at B ie, maximum velocity. The particle moves with uniform acceleration from A to B.

For motion from A to B.

Average velocity =0+v2=v2

Time taken,  t1=2xv2

Particle moves with uniform retardation from C TO D, Vavg=0+v2=v2

Time taken ,t3=6x(v2)

Total time =t1+t2+t3

t=2xv2+4xv+6xv2=20xv

Average Velocity over = 6x+2x+4x20xv=35v

Required Ratio =AverageSpeedv=35
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The motion of a particle moving along the y-axis is represented as <math><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>. Identify the correct statement
options: the initial <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> velocity of the particle is <math><mn>3 </mn><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">m </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>
the acceleration of the particle is <math><mn>5 </mn><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">m </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>
the particle is at the origin at <math><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>2 </mn><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math>
questions.solution:
We know that v→=dydt & a→=dv→dt
v=dydt=3+10t-2
At t=0 velocity v=-17 m s-1
a=dvdt=10 m s-2
y=3t-2+5t-22
At the value of y will be y=32-2+52-22
therefore y=0
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A particle starts from rest and has an acceleration of $2 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ for $10 \mathrm{sec}$. After that, it travels for $30 \mathrm{sec}$ with constant speed and then undergoes a retardation of $4 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ and comes back to rest. The total distance covered by the particle is
questions.solution:
Initial velocity $(u)=0$,
Acceleration $\left(a_1\right)=2 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ and time during acceleration $\left(t_1\right)=10 \mathrm{sec}$.
Time during constant velocity $\left(t_2\right)=30 \mathrm{sec}$ and retardation $\left(a_2\right)=-4 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ (-ve sign due to retardation). Distance covered by the particle during acceleration,
$$
\begin{gathered}
s_1=u t_1+\frac{1}{2} a_1 t_1^2=(0 \times 10)+\frac{1}{2} \times 2 \times(10)^2 \\
=100 \mathrm{~m}
\end{gathered}
$$
And velocity of the particle at the end of acceleration,
$$
v=u+a_1 t_1=0+(2 \times 10)=20 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} .
$$
Therefore distance covered by the particle during constant velocity $\left(s_2\right)=v \times t_2$
$$
=20 \times 30=600 \mathrm{~m}
$$
Relation for the distance covered by the particle during retardation $\left(s_3\right)$ is $v^2=u^2+2 a_2 s_3$
or, $\quad(0)^2=(20)^2+2 \times(-4) \times s_3=400-8 s_3$
or, $s_3=400 / 8=50 \mathrm{~m}$
Therefore total distance covered by the particle


$$
s=s_1+s_2+s_3=100+600+50=750 \mathrm{~m}
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
